---
title: "WGCNA for leaf in control, consensus test for four lines. Part III - data export for further analysis."
format: html
date: "2026-04-27"
execute:
  fig-format: png
  fig.dpi: 300
---

Built with R version `r getRversion()`

This script continues [02-wgcna-leaf-ctrl-line4-2network.qmd](02-wgcna-leaf-ctrl-line4-2network.qmd).

And is based on "Tutorial for the WGCNA package for R II. Consensus network analysis of liver expression data, female and
male mice 4. Relating consensus modules to external microarray sample information and exporting network analysis results".

# Data preparation

## Environment cleaning and set-up

```{r setup, include = FALSE}
# load required packages
# rm(list = ls())
library(WGCNA)

# The following setting is important, do not omit.
options(stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
# Allow multi-threading within WGCNA.
# Caution: skip this line if you run RStudio or other third-party R environments.
# See note above.
enableWGCNAThreads()

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
```

```{r load}
rm(list = ls())

load('../rdata-saved/wgcna/Consensus-NetworkConstruction-ctrl-man13mrg025.RData')

load("../rdata-saved/wgcna/Consensus-dataInput-MAD025.RData")
```

## Compute summary table

We (re-)calculate the module eigengenes (first principal component of a given
module - representative of the gene expression profiles in a module.) 
in the ”alphabetic” order and calculate the gene significances (correlation with trait) and
module memberships (correlation of gene expression profile and module eigengene) in each data set.

```{r recalculate ME}
# Get the number of sets in the multiExpr structure.
nSets = checkSets(multiExpr)$nSets

consMEs.unord = multiSetMEs(multiExpr, 
                            universalColors = moduleLabels, 
                            excludeGrey = TRUE)
#GS = list()
kME = list()
for (set in 1:nSets)
{
  #GS[[set]] = corAndPvalue(multiExpr[[set]]$data, Traits[[set]]$data)
  kME[[set]] = corAndPvalue(multiExpr[[set]]$data, consMEs.unord[[set]]$data)
}
```

We perform a very simple ”meta-analysis” by combining the Z scores of correlations from each set to form a
meta-Z score and the corresponding p-value.

```{r meta-z}
# GS.metaZ = (GS[[1]]$Z + GS[[2]]$Z + GS[[3]]$Z + GS[[4]]$Z)/sqrt(4)
kME.metaZ = (kME[[1]]$Z + kME[[2]]$Z + kME[[3]]$Z + kME[[4]]$Z)/sqrt(4)
# GS.metaP = 2*pnorm(abs(GS.metaZ), lower.tail = FALSE)
kME.metaP = 2*pnorm(abs(kME.metaZ), lower.tail = FALSE)
```

Next we form matrices holding the GS and kME. We use a simple re-shaping trick to put the values and the
associated p-values and meta-analysis results next to one another.

```{r kME}
probes = names(multiExpr[[1]]$data)

# GSmat = rbind(GS[[1]]$cor, GS[[2]]$cor, GS[[1]]$p, GS[[2]]$p, GS.metaZ, GS.metaP)
# nTraits = checkSets(Traits)$nGenes
# traitNames = colnames(Traits[[1]]$data)
# dim(GSmat) = c(nGenes, 6*nTraits)
# rownames(GSmat) = probes
# colnames(GSmat) = spaste(
# c("GS.set1.", "GS.set2.", "p.GS.set1.", "p.GS.set2.", "Z.GS.meta.", "p.GS.meta"),
# rep(traitNames, rep(6, nTraits)))

# Same code for kME:
## bind rows (?)
kMEmat = rbind(kME[[1]]$cor, kME[[2]]$cor, kME[[3]]$cor, kME[[4]]$cor, 
               kME[[1]]$p, kME[[2]]$p, kME[[3]]$p, kME[[4]]$p , 
               kME.metaZ, kME.metaP)

## module names
MEnames = colnames(consMEs.unord[[1]]$data)
## number of modules
nMEs = checkSets(consMEs.unord)$nGenes
## reshape
dim(kMEmat) = c(nGenes, 10*nMEs)
## change row/col names
rownames(kMEmat) = probes
colnames(kMEmat) = spaste(
c("kME.set1.", "kME.set2.", "kME.set3.", "kME.set4.", 
  "p.kME.set1.", "p.kME.set2.", "p.kME.set3.", "p.kME.set4.", 
  "Z.kME.meta.", "p.kME.meta"),
  rep(MEnames, rep(10, nMEs)))
```

## Save data

```{r export}
info = data.frame(Probe = probes,
ModuleLabel = moduleLabels,
ModuleColor = labels2colors(moduleLabels),
#GSmat,
kMEmat)
# save whole table
# save(info, file = '../rdata-saved/wgcna/genes-modules16.RDa')
write.table(info, file = "consensusAnalysis-leaf13mrg025.txt", sep = '\t', row.names = FALSE, quote = FALSE)
```


```{r df to list}
# Extract only gene ID and module assignment
# and exclude module 0 - unassigned genes
info2 = subset(info, ModuleLabel != 0, select = c(Probe, ModuleColor)) # alternatively ModuleLabel column can be selected

# Create list
genes_modules <- split(info2$Probe, info2$ModuleColor) # alternatively ModuleLabel column can be selected

# All genes after MAD-filtering, it will be population for GO-enrichment
mad.genes = as.vector(info[, 'Probe'])

save(genes_modules, info, mad.genes, file = '../rdata-saved/wgcna/modules4go13mrg025.RDa')
```

:::{.callout-note}
Other means to export only gene IDs and module color - [section 'Relate Module (cluster) Assignments to Treatment Groups'](https://bioinformaticsworkbook.org/tutorials/wgcna.html#gsc.tab=0). And for Cytoscape: 'Generate and Export Networks'.
:::


:::(.callout-note)
Below section is to be run *after* GO-enrichment analysis [02-wgcna-leaf-ctrl-line4-4go.qmd](02-wgcna-leaf-ctrl-line4-4go.qmd).
:::

## Save data (megred consensus modules) for cytoscape

Cytoscape [@shannonCytoscapeSoftwareEnvironment2003] allows the user to input an edge file and a node file, allowing the user to specify for example the link weights and the node colors.

```{r load2}
rm(list = ls())
load("../rdata-saved/wgcna/cons13merged025.RDa") # multiExpr (for gene names)
load("../rdata-saved/wgcna/after_merge025.RDa") # consensus TOM and merged ModuleColors

# Read in the annotation file
annot = read.table("../../../input-data/go-uniprot/NAM-UP-anno", sep = "\t", header = FALSE)
```

### Joint dataset for selected modules

```{r cyto multi}
## Method to gen all modules
# x = unique((mergedColors))
# 
# modules = x[x != 'grey']

# For convenience data for three sets of modules are saved - one for each main branch in merged module dendrogram

# modules = c("darkgrey", "plum1", "ivory", "paleturquoise", "brown", "turquoise")

# modules = c("skyblue", "violet", "cyan", "yellow")

# modules = c("darkgreen", "darkred", "royalblue", "greenyellow")

# Thresholds choosen to make manageable networks
# darkgrey, 0.1
# plum1, 0.1
# ivory, 0.1
# paleturquoise, 0.1
# brown, 0.2
# turquoise, 0.35
# skyblue, 0.1
# violet, 0.1
# cyan, 0.2
# yellow, 0.3
# darkgreen, 0.1
# darkred, 0.1
# royalblue, 0.1


modules = 'greenyellow'

# Select module probes
probes = colnames(multiExpr[[1]]$data) # select colnames from one of the sets
inModule = is.finite(match(moduleColors, modules))
modProbes = probes[inModule]
modProts = annot$V2[match(modProbes, annot$V1)]
# Select the corresponding Topological Overlap
modTOM = consensusTOM[inModule, inModule]
dimnames(modTOM) = list(modProbes, modProbes)
# Export the network into edge and node list files Cytoscape can read
cyt = exportNetworkToCytoscape(modTOM,
  # edgeFile = paste("../results/wgcna/network/CytoscapeInput-edges-", paste(modules, collapse="-"), ".txt", sep=""),
  # nodeFile = paste("../results/wgcna/network/CytoscapeInput-nodes-", paste(modules, collapse="-"), ".txt", sep=""),
  edgeFile = "../results/wgcna/network/CytoscapeInput-edges-greenyellow.txt",
  nodeFile = "../results/wgcna/network/CytoscapeInput-nodes-greenyellow.txt",
  weighted = TRUE,
  threshold = 0.15,
  nodeNames = modProbes,
  altNodeNames = modProts,
  nodeAttr = moduleColors[inModule])

# separately add NAM-UP-anno as Node table
```


### Dataset by module

```{r cyto one}
## Method to gen all modules
x = unique((mergedColors))

modules = x[x != 'grey']

for (module in modules) {

# Select module probes
probes = colnames(multiExpr[[1]]$data) # select colnames from one of the sets
inModule = is.finite(match(moduleColors, module))
modProbes = probes[inModule]
modProts = annot$V2[match(modProbes, annot$V1)]
# Select the corresponding Topological Overlap
modTOM = consensusTOM[inModule, inModule]
dimnames(modTOM) = list(modProbes, modProbes)
# Export the network into edge and node list files Cytoscape can read
cyt = exportNetworkToCytoscape(modTOM,
  edgeFile = paste("../results/wgcna/network/CytoscapeInput-edges-", paste(module, collapse="-"), ".txt", sep=""),
  nodeFile = paste("../results/wgcna/network/CytoscapeInput-nodes-", paste(module, collapse="-"), ".txt", sep=""),
  weighted = TRUE,
  threshold = 0.1,
  nodeNames = modProbes,
  altNodeNames = modProts,
  nodeAttr = moduleColors[inModule])

}
# separately add NAM-UP-anno as Node table
```

:::{.callout-note}
A note on "weight" from Peter Langfelder 
> The weight generated by exportNetworkToCytoscape is exactly the matrix you give it as input.

[source](https://support.bioconductor.org/p/95965/#104696)

And on threshold
> My operational answer is to select a threshold that keeps the file size manageable, then use filtering in Cytoscape to interactively choose a threshold that results in an informative plot. This assumes that Cytoscape is only used for visualization; if you're going to use Cytoscape for further analysis, you should probably set the threshold to zero.

[source](https://support.bioconductor.org/p/95965/#104724)
:::

:::{.callout-note}
Network import to Cytoscape has to be done in two steps, first *edge* file should be imported as network. To this *node* file can be added as *node attributes* table - continued in [02-wgcna-leaf-ctrl-line4-4cytoscape.qmd](02-wgcna-leaf-ctrl-line4-4cytoscape.qmd)
:::

## Reshape `info` table for module comparison between lines

The aim of this section is to extract genes comprising "darkturquoise" module in individual lines.

```{r module number}
# Identify module number - color assignement
info |> select(ModuleColor, ModuleLabel) |> unique() |> filter(ModuleColor == 'darkturquoise')

# module number 23 is needed
```

```{r check p-vals}
# Check significance of modules - p-value from correlation with module eigengene

x <- info |> 
  filter(if_any(starts_with("p."), ~ . < 0.05))


# Only 3 rows don't have p-value below threshold - leave table as is. The p-vals are not fdr-adjusted.
```

```{r}
# leave only kMEs from module 23, correlation with it, and module assignment
df = select(info, Probe:ModuleColor, matches("^kME.*23$")) |> filter(ModuleColor == 'darkturquoise')

# There are 124 genes
```

```{r check p-vals}
# Check significance of modules - p-value from correlation with module eigengene

x <- info |> 
  filter(if_any(matches("^kME.*23$"), ~ . < 0.05))
#wartość bezwzględna korelacji!

# Only 3 rows don't have p-value below threshold - leave table as is. The p-vals are not fdr-adjusted.
```


```{r}

#x = pivot_longer(df, cols = starts_with('kME'), names_sep = '\\.', names_to = c('v1', 'set', 'module'))

# szybki sposób na scattery parami
library(GGally)
library(ggplot2)

df_num = select(df, starts_with('kME'))

# podstawowy wykres par rozrzutu
ggpairs(df_num)

```
